title: Non-fatal set_diversifier_index: send address even if DB advance fails
description: When set_diversifier_index fails, the code logs a warning and continues — the generated address is still sent to the payer. A future contributor might suggest making this fatal ('if we can't record the DI, abort the payment'). Resist: a DB write failure that aborts the payment creates a worse outcome than address reuse: the payer has no address and cannot complete the payment at all. The trade-off is: rare DB error → possible address reuse (low-severity privacy issue) vs fatal → guaranteed payment failure. For a payment channel, failed payment is worse than imperfect unlinkability. The correct fix is nie-cs0.3 (advance_diversifier_to) which prevents the failure mode at the source.
close_reason: Decision reversed by nie-cs0.1: failure IS fatal when found_di > start (Sapling skipped), because sending in that case risks address reuse. Non-fatal path retained only when found_di == start (DB is already correct after next_diversifier increment).
